title: Gear + Linkage
goal: Combines meshing gears with an off-center crank hole so a rotating gear can push and pull a linkage arm.
gallery_summary: Drive a linkage from a gear-mounted crank pin for timed push-pull motion.
parts: Drive Gear: Handle-side gear receiving input
Driven Gear: Gear carrying the off-center crank hole
Linkage Arm: Bar connected to the crank hole
Spacer Stack: Keeps gear, linkage, and board from rubbing
Paper Fasteners: Loose pivots at axles and linkage joints
advantages: Uses gear ratio and linkage reach in one compact mechanism
Works naturally with fabricated gear holes and bracket pivots
Easy to prototype on the 15 by 15 pegboard
Makes timing changes visible through gear tooth counts
disadvantages: Requires careful spacer ordering to avoid rubbing
Large crank throw can collide with neighboring parts
Loose fasteners may introduce wobble in long linkages
materials: Small fabricated gears with center and crank holes
Paper fasteners for axles and joints
Straight or L bracket for the output pivot
cautions: Keep the linkage pin loose enough to rotate
Check that the bar clears gear teeth over a full turn
Use only supported gear sizes so board spacing remains compatible
